description_official: DRAFT — verify against current AFECD. Cyber Defense Operations airmen execute defensive cyberspace operations, analyzing cyber threats and responding to incidents on Air Force networks.
description_plain: Defensive cyber for the Air Force. You run intrusion detection, hunt threats on Air Force networks, and respond to incidents. Classroom-heavy pipeline, fast-evolving skill set, and the clearance you earn is a career asset for decades after you separate.
description_tldr: Air Force defensive cyber operator — monitors, hunts, and responds to threats on Air Force networks.

asvab.notes: Air Force uses four aptitude composites: Mechanical (M), Administrative (A), General (G), Electronics (E).

training_pipeline.description: DRAFT — verify current course length and structure.

compensation.typical_entry_rank: E-3 (Airman First Class) on standard contract

sources.name: AFECD — Air Force Enlisted Classification Directory (DRAFT reference)

notes: DRAFT — verify current AFSC structure, shredouts, and tech school length.

description_official: DRAFT — verify. TACP airmen are Air Force ground warfare specialists who embed with Army and Marine units to coordinate close air support.
description_plain: TACP. Air Force ground warfare. You embed with Army or Marine infantry units and call in close air support when they're in a fight. High-attrition pipeline, infantry-adjacent physical standards, and a tight community. Often conflated with Combat Controllers (different AFSC, even harder pipeline) — TACP is the most common ground-embed AFSC.
description_tldr: Air Force Tactical Air Control Party — embeds with ground units to call in and coordinate close air support.

physical_requirements.details: Passes TACP selection including PAST (Physical Ability and Stamina Test).

training_pipeline.description: Follow-on qualification for many TACP billets.

special_pay.conditions: When deployed to designated combat zones

notes: DRAFT — verify TACP pipeline and JTAC qualification currency.
